headline: Malaysia-Brunei Joint Petroleum Development Zone Produces First Oil
context: Malaysia-Brunei joint petroleum development zone in offshore waters produced first oil in January 2026 ending 16-year negotiation.

cards.big: Malaysia-Brunei joint zone produces first oil after 16-year negotiation
cards.sub: Production from 2 platforms reached 15,000 barrels per day in first quarter

cards.big: Agreement signed 2009 resolved overlapping continental shelf claims
cards.sub: Revenue shared 50-50 between Petronas and Brunei National Petroleum

cards.big: Estimated recoverable reserves of 800 million barrels over 30-year production life
cards.sub: Zone could generate RM180 billion in total revenue for both countries

cards.big: Model cited as potential template for resolving other ASEAN maritime disputes
cards.sub: Environmental monitoring includes real-time reef and marine mammal tracking

cards.big: Joint development tests whether resource sharing can resolve maritime boundary disputes permanently
First oil from the Brunei joint development zone proves that shared sovereignty can produce shared revenue.
